chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No. 1907 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES For hearing of bail application. 25-09-2025 Mr. Muhammad Nawaz Kubar, advocate a/w applicants. Mr. Ali Gohar Masroof, advocate for complainant. Mr. Muhammad Iqbal Awan, Additional Prosecutor General. ============= Omar Sial, J: Muhammad Bilal Shah and Abdullah seek pre- arrest bail in crime number 212 of 2025, registered under sections 324 and 34 of the P.P.C., at the Bahadurabad police station. The F.I.R. was registered on 23.05.2025 on information provided by Muhammad Asim. Asim reported that he was sitting in his shop when a resident of the area, Muhammad Yousuf, came there with his two sons (the two applicants) and, after an exchange of harsh words, Yousuf fired at Asim, injuring his leg. 2. I have heard the learned counsels for the applicants and the complainant, as well as the learned Additional Prosecutor General. 3. The applicants say that this is an entirely false case and that no such incident occurred. The photos provided by the complainant pertain to an older incident that had happened between the two parties. Allegedly, Asim and their family were on bad terms due to Asim teasing their sister in the neighborhood. There are bus tickets on record that, upon a tentative assessment, show that the applicants were not present when the incident is said to have occurred. No overt role has been assigned to either applicant. Whether the incident occurred and if it did, whether the applicants shared a common intention with their father, Yousuf, requires further inquiry. 2 Malafide, in the background of the parties' past relationship, cannot conclusively be ruled out at this preliminary stage. 4.

No overt role has been assigned to either applicant. Whether the incident occurred and if it did, whether the applicants shared a common intention with their father, Yousuf, requires further inquiry. 2 Malafide, in the background of the parties' past relationship, cannot conclusively be ruled out at this preliminary stage. 4. Given the above, the interim pre-arrest bail earlier granted to the applicants is confirmed on the same terms and conditions, JUDGE
